message:
feat(extensions): add unvendored extension fetch+probe pipeline and validation docs

Introduce ext_unvendored_fetch_run binary for multi-threaded acquisition
and runtime probing of unvendored extension candidates from npm and
GitHub sources:

CLI subcommands:
- run-all: multi-threaded pipeline that fetches extension candidates,
  detects entrypoints via heuristic scoring (API imports, registration
  patterns, filename conventions), and optionally probes each with the
  real QuickJS runtime. Produces JSON report + JSONL event stream.
- probe-one: probes a single entrypoint with the JS extension runtime,
  reporting registered commands/tools/flags/providers

Pipeline features:
- Candidate pool filtering and priority ranking
- Code-search summary hints for pre-screening
- npm pack + tarball extraction with retry
- Shallow git clone with retry for GitHub sources
- Filesystem scanning with configurable file limits
- Structured JSON/JSONL reporting

Document the complete extension validation pipeline in README.md covering
fetch, E2E orchestration stages (onboarding queue refresh, conformance
sharded matrix, failure dossiers, provider compat, scenario conformance,
auto-repair), artifact paths, and CI integration commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
